name: National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency
mission: Provides timely, relevant, and accurate geospatial intelligence in support of national security.

name: National Reconnaissance Office
mission: Designs, builds, and operates the nation's reconnaissance satellites.

name: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
mission: Leads integration of foreign, military, and domestic intelligence across the IC.

name: Defense Intelligence Agency
mission: Combat support agency producing military intelligence for warfighters, defense policymakers, and force planners.

name: Intelligence Advanced Research Projects Activity
mission: Invests in high-risk, high-payoff research to tackle the hardest challenges facing the IC.

name: Joint Special Operations Command
mission: Studies special operations requirements and techniques, ensures interoperability, develops joint training exercises.

name: Special Activities Center (CIA)
mission: Covert paramilitary operations and highly sensitive intelligence collection activities.

name: Office of Naval Intelligence
mission: Provides maritime intelligence to support naval operations and national defense.

name: Central Intelligence Agency
mission: Foreign intelligence collection, analysis, and covert action.

name: United States Cyber Command
mission: Directs, synchronizes, and coordinates cyberspace operations and defends DoD networks.

name: National Security Agency
mission: Signals intelligence and information assurance for the nation.

name: Central Security Service
mission: Prescribes, produces, and enables cryptology for the DoD.

name: Army Cyber Command
mission: Directs and conducts integrated electronic warfare and cyberspace operations.

name: Fleet Cyber Command
mission: Executes the full spectrum of cyberspace operations for the Navy.

name: Sixteenth Air Force (Air Forces Cyber)
mission: Integrates multi-domain ISR, cyber, electronic warfare, and information operations.

name: Space Delta 6 (USSF Cyber)
mission: Provides cyberspace operations for U.S. Space Force.

name: Joint Force Headquarters — Cyber
mission: Plans and executes cyber operations in support of combatant commands.

name: U.S. Space Systems Command
mission: Acquires and delivers resilient, affordable, and mission-ready space capabilities.

name: Space Development Agency
mission: Rapidly develops and delivers a proliferated warfighter space architecture.

name: Missile Defense Agency
mission: Develops, tests, and fields an integrated, layered missile defense system.

name: National Space Intelligence Center
mission: Provides all-source intelligence on foreign space and counterspace capabilities.

name: Air Force Research Laboratory
mission: Leading the discovery, development, and integration of affordable warfighting technologies.

name: Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency
mission: Makes pivotal investments in breakthrough technologies for national security.

name: National Aeronautics and Space Administration
mission: Exploring the universe for the benefit of all humanity.

name: U.S. Strategic Command
mission: Deters strategic attack and employs forces as directed to guarantee U.S. and allied freedom of action.

name: Air Force Global Strike Command
mission: Organizes, trains, equips, and maintains two-thirds of the nuclear triad.

name: Naval Strategic Systems Programs
mission: Develops and maintains the Navy's fleet ballistic missile and strategic weapons system.

name: National Nuclear Security Administration
mission: Enhances national security through the military application of nuclear science.

name: Defense Threat Reduction Agency
mission: Safeguards America and its allies from weapons of mass destruction.

name: U.S. Marshals Service
mission: Enforcement arm of the federal courts and the nation's oldest federal law enforcement agency.

name: Diplomatic Security Service
mission: Provides a safe and secure environment for the conduct of U.S. foreign policy.

name: Federal Protective Service
mission: Protects federal facilities, their occupants, and visitors.

name: Office of Inspector General (DOJ)
mission: Detects and deters waste, fraud, abuse, and misconduct in DOJ programs.

name: Bureau of Industry and Security
mission: Advances U.S. national security and foreign policy goals through export controls.

name: Office of Foreign Assets Control
mission: Administers and enforces economic and trade sanctions programs.

name: Financial Crimes Enforcement Network
mission: Safeguards the financial system from illicit use and combats money laundering.

name: IRS Criminal Investigation
mission: Investigates potential criminal violations of the Internal Revenue Code and related financial crimes.

name: U.S. Secret Service
mission: Protects national leaders and investigates financial crimes.

name: United States Mint
mission: Produces circulating coinage for commerce and commemorative coins.

name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency
mission: Leads the national effort to understand, manage, and reduce risk to cyber and physical infrastructure.

name: Transportation Security Administration
mission: Protects the nation's transportation systems to ensure freedom of movement for people and commerce.

name: Federal Emergency Management Agency
mission: Helps people before, during, and after disasters.

name: U.S. Customs and Border Protection
mission: Safeguards America's borders, protecting the public from dangerous people and materials.

name: Immigration and Customs Enforcement
mission: Enforces federal laws governing border control, customs, trade, and immigration.

name: National Institute of Standards and Technology
mission: Promotes U.S. innovation and industrial competitiveness through measurement science.

name: Sandia National Laboratories
mission: Develops science-based technologies that support national security.

name: Los Alamos National Laboratory
mission: Delivers science and technology to ensure the safety, security, and reliability of the U.S. nuclear deterrent.

name: Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory
mission: Strengthens the United States' security through development and application of world-class science.

name: National Science Foundation
mission: Promotes the progress of science; advances the national health, prosperity, and welfare.
